title: conyza varia
intro: Conyza varia, also known as wild tobacco or horse mint, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the daisy family. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as sore throats, colds, and bronchial infections, due to its expectorant and decongestant properties. Conyza varia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy among herbalists and traditional healers.
